name: Sans Souci Beach (Queen's Beach)
tagline: Waikiki's secret reef — locals snorkel here while tourists stay on the sand a mile west.
parking: Free street parking on Kalakaua Ave or paid lot at Kapiolani Park; metered spots fill early
bestTime: Early morning for calmest water and best visibility; year-round spot but summer is ideal
talkStory: Named after the resort where Robert Louis Stevenson stayed in 1893, calling these waters 'the most restful spot on earth.' The reef hosts resident schools of aholehole (Hawaiian flagtail), a culturally significant fish used in ceremonies at nearby heiau. The crumbling Natatorium war memorial creates a current break that funnels nutrients and attracts green sea turtles to its seawall. Locals enter the sandy channel and drift right where coral coverage is densest.
